title: Standard operational and management reports library
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a library of standard reports covering workforce management, organization structure, leave and attendance, talent acquisition, lifecycle workflows, asset management, and policy compliance, so that I can meet routine operational, management, compliance, and analytical reporting needs without building reports from scratch.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am an authorized Company Admin, when I open the reporting area, then a catalog of standard reports is available spanning every listed domain: workforce management, organization structure, leave and attendance, talent acquisition, lifecycle workflows, asset management, and policy compliance.; Given I select any standard report, when I run it, then it returns data scoped strictly to the company/companies I have access to.; Given a standard report is displayed, when I choose to export it, then the report can be output for sharing in the supported formats.; Given the report supports parameters, when I set report parameters (such as date range or department), then the output reflects the selected parameters.; Given a standard report exists, when the underlying operational data changes, then a subsequent run reflects the current data.

title: Workforce management and organization structure reports
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want workforce management and organization structure reports, so that I can understand headcount, workforce composition, and the reporting hierarchy across the company.
acceptance_criteria: Given workforce data exists, when I run the workforce management report, then current employee and headcount information is presented for the applicable scope.; Given an organization hierarchy is defined, when I run the organization structure report, then the reporting structure and hierarchy are presented.; Given I have access to only certain companies, when I run either report, then only in-scope employees and organization units are shown.; Given the report is displayed, when I export it, then the workforce/org data is output for sharing.

title: Leave, attendance, and asset management reports
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want standard leave and attendance and asset management reports, so that I can monitor my team's time records and assigned assets.
acceptance_criteria: Given leave and attendance data exists, when I run the leave and attendance report, then relevant records are displayed for the applicable scope and selected period.; Given assets are assigned, when I run the asset management report, then asset allocation details (asset, assignee, status) are displayed.; Given I have restricted company access, when I run either report, then only in-scope records are returned.; Given a report is displayed, when I export it, then the records are output for sharing.

title: Interactive dashboards with charts KPIs and drill-down
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want interactive dashboards with charts, KPIs, and drill-down capabilities, so that I can monitor key metrics visually and investigate details on demand.
acceptance_criteria: Given I open a dashboard, when it loads, then it displays charts and KPIs summarizing the relevant data for my scope.; Given a KPI or chart element is shown, when I drill down into it, then more granular underlying data is displayed.; Given a dashboard is interactive, when I change a filter or dimension, then the charts and KPIs update accordingly.; Given I have restricted company access, when the dashboard loads, then only in-scope data contributes to the charts and KPIs.

title: Role-based default dashboards
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the system to present role-based default dashboards, so that each user sees metrics relevant to their role upon login.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user logs in, when their dashboard loads, then the default dashboard shown corresponds to their role.; Given users have different roles, when they view their dashboards, then the default dashboard content differs according to each role's relevance.; Given a role-based default dashboard is defined, when a user of that role first accesses dashboards, then the appropriate default is applied without manual setup.; Given a user has restricted data access, when their role-based default dashboard loads, then only data within their access scope is shown.

title: Ad hoc report builder with field selection
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want an ad hoc report builder where I can select fields, so that I can construct custom reports tailored to specific questions.
acceptance_criteria: Given I open the report builder, when I choose fields to include, then only the selected fields appear in the report output.; Given I have selected fields, when I run the report, then results reflect exactly my field selections.; Given I am building an ad hoc report, when I add or remove fields, then the output columns update accordingly.; Given I have restricted company access, when I run an ad hoc report, then only in-scope records are available for selection and output.

title: Ad hoc filtering and grouping
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to apply filtering and grouping in the ad hoc report builder, so that I can narrow and organize results meaningfully.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am building an ad hoc report, when I apply filters, then only records matching the filter criteria are returned.; Given I am building an ad hoc report, when I apply grouping, then results are organized by the chosen grouping dimension.; Given I combine multiple filters, when I run the report, then all filter conditions are applied together.; Given grouping is applied, when the report renders, then group-level organization (and any subtotals) is presented.

title: Saved ad hoc report views
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to save my ad hoc report configurations as views, so that I can reuse them without rebuilding.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have configured an ad hoc report, when I save it as a view, then the field, filter, and grouping configuration is retained.; Given a saved view exists, when I reopen it, then the report is reconstructed with its saved configuration.; Given a saved view exists, when I run it later, then it returns current data using the saved configuration.; Given I own saved views, when I manage them, then I can rename, update, or delete a saved view.

title: Scheduled report delivery via email
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to schedule reports for delivery via email with configurable frequency options, so that stakeholders receive reports automatically without manual effort.
acceptance_criteria: Given a report, when I configure a schedule, then I can set a delivery frequency from the available frequency options (such as daily, weekly, monthly).; Given I configure a schedule, when I set recipients, then the report is delivered to the configured recipient list.; Given a scheduled report is due, when the scheduled time arrives, then the report is generated and delivered via email to the configured recipients.; Given a delivered scheduled report, when it is generated, then it respects the sender's company access scope.; Given an existing schedule, when I edit or cancel it, then future deliveries reflect the change.

title: Statutory compliance reports and registers
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want compliance reports including PF/ESIC eligibility, attendance registers, leave registers, and wage register templates, so that I can satisfy statutory reporting obligations.
acceptance_criteria: Given eligibility data exists, when I run the PF/ESIC eligibility report, then eligible employees are listed with their eligibility status.; Given attendance data exists, when I run the attendance register, then the register is produced in the expected statutory format.; Given leave data exists, when I run the leave register, then the register is produced in the expected statutory format.; Given payroll data exists, when I generate the wage register, then it is produced using the wage register template.; Given a compliance report is produced, when I export it, then the output is in a format suitable for statutory submission.

title: Statutory data completeness reporting
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want statutory data completeness reports, so that I can identify missing required data before statutory filing.
acceptance_criteria: Given employee records exist, when I run the statutory data completeness report, then records with missing statutory data are flagged.; Given completeness gaps are flagged, when I review the report, then I can identify which specific fields or records need to be completed.; Given data is subsequently completed, when I re-run the report, then previously flagged records no longer appear as incomplete.; Given I have restricted company access, when I run the report, then only in-scope records are evaluated for completeness.

title: Company-scoped report security with cross-company access
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want reports filtered by company access while allowing cross-company reporting for authorized portfolio/group company users, so that data confidentiality is preserved while authorized users get consolidated views.
acceptance_criteria: Given a Company/HR Admin runs a report, when results are returned, then only data for companies they have access to is included.; Given a user is an authorized Portfolio Admin or Group Company Admin, when they run a cross-company report, then data spanning their authorized companies is included.; Given a user lacks access to a company, when a report is run, then that company's data is excluded from the results.; Given report access rules are configured, when any report or dashboard runs, then the same company-access filter is enforced consistently across standard, ad hoc, scheduled, and compliance reports.

title: Talent acquisition lifecycle and policy compliance reports
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want standard talent acquisition, employee lifecycle workflow, and policy compliance reports, so that I can track recruitment progress, workflow status, and policy adherence.
acceptance_criteria: Given talent acquisition data exists, when I run the talent acquisition report, then recruitment/hiring pipeline information is presented for the applicable scope.; Given lifecycle workflows are in progress, when I run the lifecycle workflows report, then the status and progress of onboarding/transfer/exit workflows are displayed.; Given policy compliance data exists, when I run the policy compliance report, then policy acknowledgement/compliance status is presented.; Given I have restricted company access, when I run these reports, then only in-scope records are returned.; Given a report is displayed, when I export it, then the data is output for sharing.

title: Self-service dashboard for own data
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want a self-service dashboard showing my own metrics, so that I can view my leave balances, attendance, and relevant personal information at a glance.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am an Employee (User) with self-service access, when I log in, then my role-based default dashboard displays KPIs and charts scoped to my own records.; Given my dashboard displays a metric, when I drill down, then only my own underlying data is shown.; Given I am an Employee (User), when my dashboard loads, then I cannot see other employees' data or company-wide aggregates beyond my scope.

title: Self-service reports on own records
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to run self-service reports limited to my own records, so that I can review my personal leave, attendance, and asset information without seeing others' data.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am an Employee (User) with self-service access, when I run a self-service report, then only my own records are returned.; Given I attempt to view another employee's or company-wide data, when a report is run, then that data is excluded and access is denied beyond my own scope.; Given I run a self-service report, when it is displayed, then I can export my own data for personal use.

title: Inclusion of non-user employees in registers and compliance reports
user_story: As an Employee (Non-User) whose data is maintained by HR, I want to be included in registers and compliance reports even though I have no login, so that statutory registers and completeness checks cover the entire workforce.
acceptance_criteria: Given non-user employee records exist, when an admin runs attendance, leave, or wage registers, then those non-user employees are included where applicable.; Given non-user employees have statutory attributes, when the PF/ESIC eligibility and statutory data completeness reports run, then non-user employee records are evaluated and flagged for missing data like any other employee.; Given a non-user employee has no login, when reports are generated, then their data is still reportable by authorized admins without requiring the employee to log in.

title: Cross-company consolidated reporting for portfolio
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want cross-company reporting and consolidated dashboards spanning all companies in my portfolio, so that I can get an enterprise-wide view while still respecting authorization boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am an authorized Portfolio Admin, when I run a cross-company report, then data spanning all companies within my authorized portfolio is included and consolidated.; Given a consolidated dashboard, when it loads, then KPIs and charts aggregate across my authorized companies with the ability to drill down to a specific company.; Given a company falls outside my authorized portfolio, when I run any report, then that company's data is excluded.; Given I run a cross-company report, when results are returned, then I can group or segment the results by company.

title: Cross-company reporting within group company
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want cross-company reporting and dashboards spanning the companies within my group company, so that I can produce consolidated group-level reports for authorized companies only.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am an authorized Group Company Admin, when I run a cross-company report, then data spanning the companies within my group company is included.; Given a company is outside my group company authorization, when I run a report, then that company's data is excluded.; Given a group-level consolidated report, when I run it, then I can view aggregated results and drill down to individual companies within my group.; Given a group-level dashboard, when it loads, then KPIs aggregate across my authorized group companies only.

title: Point-in-time and historical (as-of) reporting from bitemporal data
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want reports to draw on the bitemporal, effective-dated data model so that I can run point-in-time (as-of a chosen date) reports and see historically accurate values rather than only the current state.
acceptance_criteria: Given the data model records effective-dated history, when I run a report as of a past date, then values reflect what was in effect on that date rather than current values.; Given a record was later corrected, when I run an as-of report, then the output reflects the data as it was known and effective for the selected point in time.; Given time-series reporting across periods, when I compare periods, then each period draws from the correct effective-dated snapshot.; Given I have restricted company access, when an as-of report runs, then only records in scope for that period are returned.

title: Tenant-scoped row-level data isolation underlying all report queries
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want every report query to execute against tenant-scoped, row-level-secured storage so that company-access filtering is enforced at the data layer regardless of how a report is built.
acceptance_criteria: Given reports read from tenant-scoped storage, when any standard, ad hoc, dashboard, scheduled, or compliance query executes, then row-level security restricts returned rows to the requester's authorized companies at the data layer.; Given a user attempts to widen scope via ad hoc field selection or filters, when the query runs, then RLS still prevents out-of-scope rows from being returned.; Given cross-company authorization, when an authorized portfolio/group user queries, then RLS admits exactly their authorized companies' rows and no others.; Given an access grant is revoked, when a subsequent report runs, then the newly out-of-scope company's rows are no longer returned.

title: Governed versioned effective-dated statutory report and register templates
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want statutory register and report templates (wage register, attendance register, leave register, PF/ESIC formats) maintained as per-tenant governed config that is versioned and effective-dated, so that statutory format changes can be applied without code changes.
acceptance_criteria: Given statutory templates are governed config, when I update a wage, attendance, or leave register template, then reports generated after the change use the new template without a code deployment.; Given templates are effective-dated, when a report is generated for a given period, then the template version in effect for that period is applied.; Given jurisdiction rule-packs drive statutory formats, when jurisdiction rules change, then the corresponding register and eligibility templates update via config.; Given template versions exist, when I review history, then prior template versions and their effective dates are retained.

title: Configurable per-tenant role-based dashboard and report-catalog definitions
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want role-based default dashboards and the standard report catalog defined as per-tenant governed config, so that which dashboards and reports each role sees can be changed without code.
acceptance_criteria: Given dashboard defaults are config, when I map a role to a default dashboard layout, then users of that role receive that dashboard without manual setup.; Given the report catalog is config, when I enable or disable a standard report for the tenant, then it appears or disappears from the catalog accordingly.; Given config is versioned and effective-dated, when I change a default dashboard, then the change applies to subsequent logins and the prior config is retained.; Given a user's role changes, when they next open dashboards, then the default dashboard for the new role is applied per config.

title: Metadata-driven field catalog including custom fields for the ad hoc builder
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the ad hoc report builder's available fields to be driven by the tenant's field metadata schema, including custom/UDF fields, so that newly configured fields become reportable without code changes.
acceptance_criteria: Given fields are defined in the tenant metadata schema, when I open the report builder, then the selectable field list is derived from that metadata schema.; Given a custom or UDF field is configured for the tenant, when I build an ad hoc report, then that custom field is available for selection, filtering, and grouping.; Given a field is deprecated or hidden in config, when I open the builder, then it no longer appears as selectable.; Given I have restricted access, when the field catalog loads, then only fields permitted for my scope are exposed.

title: Notification engine executes scheduled report generation and email delivery
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want scheduled reports to be generated and delivered by the shared Notification/Template engine reading the schedule config, so that delivery, templating, and retries are handled consistently by the engine.
acceptance_criteria: Given a schedule is configured, when its due time arrives, then the Notification/Template engine generates the report and dispatches it via email to the configured recipients using the applicable template.; Given the engine renders the email, when delivery runs, then the message uses the configured notification template and honors the sender's company access scope.; Given a delivery fails, when the engine processes it, then failure handling and retry occur per engine behavior and the outcome is recorded.; Given a schedule is edited or cancelled, when the engine next evaluates schedules, then it delivers per the updated schedule.

title: Rules engine evaluates statutory eligibility via decision tables for compliance reports
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want PF/ESIC eligibility and statutory completeness in compliance reports to be evaluated by the shared Rules engine using configured decision tables, so that eligibility logic changes are config-driven and consistent.
acceptance_criteria: Given eligibility decision tables are configured, when the PF/ESIC eligibility report runs, then the Rules engine evaluates each employee against the decision tables to determine eligibility status.; Given decision tables are versioned and effective-dated, when a report runs for a period, then the rule version in effect for that period is applied.; Given completeness rules are configured, when the statutory data completeness report runs, then the Rules engine flags records that fail the required-field rules.; Given a decision table is updated via config, when the report re-runs, then results reflect the new rules without code changes.

title: Category-grouped report catalog navigation
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the standard report catalog organized into browsable categories such as Employee Management, Recruitment, Attendance Management, Resource Management, Time Management, Leave Management, Performance Management, Training Management, Learning Management, Benefit Management, Travel Management, Asset Tracking, and Tax Planning, so that I can quickly locate the report I need instead of scanning a flat list.
acceptance_criteria: Given I open the reporting area, when the page loads, then reports are grouped under their category headings.; Given many reports exist, when I browse a category, then only the reports belonging to that category are listed under it.; Given I select a report within a category, when I click it, then that specific report opens.; Given I have restricted company access, when I browse any category, then only reports permitted for my scope are shown.

title: Attrition and retention analytics reports
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want dedicated attrition, retention, and joinee-versus-exit reports, so that I can measure workforce turnover and stability over time.
acceptance_criteria: Given exit and headcount data exists, when I run the Attrition Report, then attrition rate and counts are presented for the selected period and scope.; Given I run the Retention Report, when it renders, then retention metrics for the selected cohort/period are shown.; Given I run the Joinee vs Exit Report, when it renders, then joiners and leavers are compared over the selected period.; Given I select a date range or department, when I run these reports, then the metrics reflect the chosen parameters.
source: Kensium: Organization > Reports > Attrition / Retention / Joinee vs Exit

title: Employee exit analytics suite
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a suite of exit reports including Reason Wise Exit, Employee Exit, Exit Summary, and Exit Questionnaire, so that I can analyze why employees leave and act on exit feedback.
acceptance_criteria: Given exit records exist, when I run the Reason Wise Exit Report, then exits are grouped and counted by exit reason.; Given I run the Employee Exit Report, when it renders, then individual exiting employees and their exit details are listed.; Given I run the Exit Summary Report, when it renders, then a summarized view of exits for the period is shown.; Given exit questionnaires were completed, when I run the Exit Questionnaire Report, then captured exit-interview responses are presented.

title: Attendance exception and defaulters reporting
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want attendance defaulters, attendance status, and out-time detail reports, so that I can identify non-compliant attendance and take corrective action.
acceptance_criteria: Given attendance data exists, when I run the Defaulters Attendance Report, then employees with attendance exceptions are listed for the selected period.; Given I run the Attendance Status Report, when it renders, then each employee's attendance status is shown.; Given I run the Out Time Details Report, when it renders, then out-time punch details are presented.; Given I filter by department or date range, when I run these reports, then results reflect the selected parameters.

title: Comp off summary and detail reports
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want Comp Off Summary and Comp Off Details reports, so that I can track compensatory-off accrual and usage across the workforce.
acceptance_criteria: Given comp-off records exist, when I run the Comp Off Summary Report, then aggregated comp-off balances and usage are shown per employee.; Given I run the Comp Off Details Report, when it renders, then individual comp-off earn and redeem transactions are listed.; Given I select a period or department, when I run either report, then results reflect the chosen parameters.; Given I export the report, when export completes, then the comp-off data is output for sharing.
source: Kensium: Organization > Reports > Comp Off Summary / Details

title: Overtime summary and detail reports
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want Over Time Summary and Over Time Details reports, so that I can monitor overtime hours for cost control and compliance.
acceptance_criteria: Given overtime data exists, when I run the Over Time Summary Report, then aggregated overtime hours per employee are shown.; Given I run the Over Time Details Report, when it renders, then individual overtime entries with dates and hours are listed.; Given I filter by department or date range, when I run either report, then results reflect the selected parameters.; Given I export the report, when export completes, then the overtime data is output for sharing.
source: Kensium: Organization > Reports > Over Time Summary / Details

title: Work from home summary and detail reports
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want Work From Home Summary and Work From Home Details reports, so that I can track remote-work patterns across teams.
acceptance_criteria: Given WFH records exist, when I run the Work From Home Summary Report, then aggregated WFH days per employee are shown.; Given I run the Work From Home Details Report, when it renders, then individual WFH day records are listed.; Given I filter by department or date range, when I run either report, then results reflect the selected parameters.; Given I export the report, when export completes, then the WFH data is output for sharing.
source: Kensium: Organization > Reports > Work From Home Summary / Details

title: Loss of pay and leave credit reports
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want LOP, Leave Credits, and Optional Holiday reports in addition to leave summary and details, so that I can reconcile unpaid leave and leave balances before payroll.
acceptance_criteria: Given leave data exists, when I run the LOP Report, then loss-of-pay days per employee for the period are listed.; Given I run the Leave Credits Report, when it renders, then credited leave balances per employee are shown.; Given I run the Optional Holiday Report, when it renders, then optional-holiday elections and usage are presented.; Given payroll cutoff approaches, when I run the LOP Report for the pay period, then it reflects current approved leave data.

title: Resource bench billability and utilization reports
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want Bench Summary, Detailed Bench, Resource Billability, and Resource Utilization reports, so that I can manage staffing efficiency and billable capacity.
acceptance_criteria: Given resource allocation data exists, when I run the Bench Summary Report, then counts of benched resources are summarized by unit.; Given I run the Detailed Bench Report, when it renders, then each benched resource and their bench duration are listed.; Given I run the Resource Billability Report, when it renders, then billable versus non-billable status per resource is shown.; Given I run the Resource Utilization Report, when it renders, then utilization percentages per resource are presented for the selected period.

title: Timesheet and billing reports
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want Detailed Timesheet, Task Wise Billing, Detailed Billing, and Utilization Summary reports, so that I can reconcile logged time against billing.
acceptance_criteria: Given timesheet data exists, when I run the Detailed Timesheet Report, then time entries per employee, project, and task are listed.; Given I run the Task Wise Billing Report, when it renders, then billing is broken down by task.; Given I run the Detailed Billing Report, when it renders, then billable amounts per project/resource are presented.; Given I run the Utilization Summary Report, when it renders, then summarized utilization across resources is shown for the period.

title: Performance analytics reports
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want Employee Performance, Quadrant Rating, Project Wise Average Performance, and Resource Quarter Wise Performance reports, so that I can evaluate performance across individuals, projects, and periods.
acceptance_criteria: Given performance-review data exists, when I run the Employee Performance Report, then individual performance ratings are presented for the selected review period.; Given I run the Quadrant Rating Report, when it renders, then employees are distributed across the rating quadrants.; Given I run the Project Wise Average Performance Report, when it renders, then average performance per project is shown.; Given I run the Resource Quarter Wise Performance Report, when it renders, then performance is presented by quarter.

title: Diversity and gender ratio reports
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want Male Female Ratio and Gender reports, so that I can monitor workforce diversity and report on gender composition.
acceptance_criteria: Given employee gender data exists, when I run the Male Female Ratio Report, then the ratio of male to female employees is presented for the selected scope.; Given I run the Gender Report, when it renders, then headcount by gender is shown.; Given I filter by department or location, when I run either report, then the composition reflects the selected parameters.; Given I export the report, when export completes, then the diversity data is output for sharing.
source: Kensium: Organization > Reports > Male Female Ratio / Gender

title: Compensation movement reports
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want CTC Revision History, Increment, Bonus, and Promotion reports, so that I can audit compensation changes and reward decisions.
acceptance_criteria: Given compensation data exists, when I run the CTC Revision History Report, then each employee's CTC revisions with effective dates are listed.; Given I run the Increment Report, when it renders, then increments granted in the selected period are shown.; Given I run the Bonus Report, when it renders, then bonus amounts per employee are presented.; Given I run the Promotion Report, when it renders, then promotions with old and new designations are listed.

title: Training program cost and trainer reports
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want Training Cost, Training Program Details, Trainer Details, and overall Training reports, so that I can track training investment and delivery.
acceptance_criteria: Given training data exists, when I run the Training Cost Report, then training spend is presented for the selected period and scope.; Given I run the Training Program Details Report, when it renders, then program details and participants are listed.; Given I run the Trainer Details Report, when it renders, then trainers and their sessions are shown.; Given I run the Training Report, when it renders, then a consolidated training overview is presented.

title: Certification and learning reports
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want Employee Wise Certification and Certification Wise Employees reports, so that I can track certifications from both the employee and certification perspectives.
acceptance_criteria: Given certification data exists, when I run the Employee Wise Certification Report, then each employee's certifications are listed.; Given I run the Certification Wise Employees Report, when it renders, then employees are grouped under each certification.; Given a certification approaches expiry, when I run these reports, then current certification status is reflected.; Given I export the report, when export completes, then the certification data is output for sharing.

title: Monthly TDS and tax planning report
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the Employee Monthly TDS Report, so that I can review tax deducted at source per employee for payroll and statutory reconciliation.
acceptance_criteria: Given payroll and tax data exists, when I run the Employee Monthly TDS Report, then TDS deducted per employee for the selected month is listed.; Given I select a different month, when I run the report, then TDS figures for that month are shown.; Given I have restricted company access, when I run the report, then only in-scope employees' TDS is returned.; Given I export the report, when export completes, then the TDS data is output for statutory reconciliation.
source: Kensium: Organization > Reports > Tax Planning > Employee Monthly TDS

title: Talent inventory reports for skills qualifications and experience
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want Skills, Qualification, Work Experience, Language, and Aging reports, so that I can maintain a searchable inventory of workforce capabilities.
acceptance_criteria: Given employee profile data exists, when I run the Skills Report, then employees and their skills are listed.; Given I run the Qualification Report, when it renders, then educational qualifications per employee are shown.; Given I run the Work Experience Report, when it renders, then prior and current experience is presented.; Given I run the Language or Aging Report, when it renders, then language proficiency or age-band distribution is shown respectively.

title: Application login status and user adoption report
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the Application Login Status Report, so that I can see which employees have active logins and monitor self-service adoption.
acceptance_criteria: Given employee accounts exist, when I run the Application Login Status Report, then each employee's login/activation status is listed.; Given some employees are non-users, when the report runs, then their no-login status is reflected distinctly from active users.; Given I filter by department or location, when I run the report, then results reflect the selected parameters.; Given I export the report, when export completes, then the login-status data is output for sharing.
source: Kensium: Organization > Reports > Application Login Status

title: Real-time employee current status availability screen
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a real-time Employee Current Status screen filterable by location, department, project, and employee name, so that I can assess who is currently available, on leave, or on-site at a glance.
acceptance_criteria: Given I open the Employee Current Status screen, when I select a location and search, then employees at that location with their current status are listed.; Given I filter by department, when I search, then results are scoped to that department showing Department, Position Level, Employee Name, and Current Status.; Given I enter a project or employee name, when I search, then matching employees and their current status are returned.; Given I have applied filters, when I click Reset, then all filters clear to defaults for a new search.; Given I have restricted company access, when I search, then only in-scope employees are shown.

title: Asset tracking and custodian reports
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want Asset, Custodian, and Letter reports, so that I can track asset custody and issued letters across the workforce.
acceptance_criteria: Given asset data exists, when I run the Asset Report, then assets with their assignees and status are listed.; Given I run the Custodian Report, when it renders, then custodians and the assets they hold are shown.; Given I run the Letter Report, when it renders, then letters issued to employees are listed with type and date.; Given I have restricted company access, when I run these reports, then only in-scope assets and letters are returned.
source: Kensium: Organization > Reports > Asset Tracking / Custodian / Letter

title: Travel details report
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the Travel Details Report, so that I can review employee business travel for cost tracking and approvals reconciliation.
acceptance_criteria: Given travel records exist, when I run the Travel Details Report, then travel entries per employee with dates and purpose are listed.; Given I select a date range or department, when I run the report, then results reflect the selected parameters.; Given I have restricted company access, when I run the report, then only in-scope travel records are returned.; Given I export the report, when export completes, then the travel data is output for sharing.
